summary_one_line: Input must parse into at least one stored cookie (c->numcookies != 0), which requires a well-formed Set-Cookie header reaching Curl_cookie_add.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must drive curl through the cookie-parsing path so that at least one cookie is successfully added (c->numcookies > 0 at cookie_output time). This requires structured magic-byte prefix bytes plus a literal 'HTTP/' response token and a literal 'Set-Cookie' header with a parseable name=value and domain, so the false branch (numcookies==0) is escaped.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry a stable structured prefix (00 01 00 00 00 19) followed by 'pop' scheme, a host:port like '9001', the literal 'HTTP/' at offset 0x24, and a 'Set-Cookie' header — Curl_cookie_add fires 20 times (W=20 L=0) so c->numcookies becomes non-zero and line 1690 takes the true branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds at the same offsets carry scattered, unconcentrated bytes (mostly '%','A','+','.' filler) instead of the 'HTTP/' / 'Set-Cookie' literals; Curl_cookie_add and get_netscape_format never execute (L hits = 0), so no cookie is stored and line 1690 stays on the false branch in all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact compared operand (e.g. the 'HTTP/' / cookie-header literals) into the input at matching offsets, solving the literal-byte gates that gate cookie parsing. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace; value_profile's Hamming-closeness feedback only rewards approaching operands and cannot synthesize the exact multi-byte literals needed, so both losers stall.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of if(c->numcookies) in cookie_output.
Both decisive pairs differ by exactly the I2S technique and both winners resolve 10/10 while losers block 10/10.
Winner inputs execute Curl_cookie_add and get_netscape_format 20 times each while losers never execute them.
On the blocker line the winner takes the true side and the loser takes the false side, confirming c->numcookies divergence.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1690  T=20 F=0  T=0 F=20  if(c->numcookies) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry the literal 'HTTP/' token whereas loser seeds carry scattered filler bytes at the same offsets.
Winner seeds end in the literal Set-Cookie header bytes (53 65 74 2d 43 6f 6f = 'Set-Coo').
cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which substitutes the exact CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so its loser cannot synthesize the required literals.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 62 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2b428b2993f47feb` and observed 2 I2S-floor rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 14 and depth 15 of the ancestor chain; the tool's trailing 'I2S-floor signal' line confirmed dash rows characteristic of unwrapped I2SRandReplace finds in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating c->numcookies on a multi-byte literal where naive/value_profile resolve the true branch as fast as cmplog/vpc — showing the literal-byte gate is not what separates the I2S-equipped winners from the losers.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact set of literal CMP operands I2S must substitute (HTTP/ vs Set-Cookie vs scheme/port) is inferred from byte-diff concentration, not from a CMP-operand dump, so the precise gating comparison inside Curl_cookie_add is uncertain.
